title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Command Brief: Signals Escalation
summary: Diagnose a worker that appears hung while preserving evidence, avoiding collateral termination, and distinguishing shell jobs from system processes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux signals escalation increment in a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup.
Use ps, pgrep, pidof, top-like evidence, and proc data with filters that identify the intended process uniquely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Signals Escalation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2205; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Signals Escalation contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup.

content.text: Send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2205; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup. A defensible solution applies Signals Escalation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook.

content.code:
pgrep -a -P 2100
ps -o pid,ppid,stat,ni,cmd -p 2201
kill -TERM 2201
# Verify exit and cleanup before considering a stronger signal.
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
